name: Urabe no Suetake
description: Urabe no Suetake, a valiant warrior and loyal retainer of Minamoto no Yorimitsu (also known as Raiko), is a prominent figure in Japanese folklore and warrior tales. He is renowned for his exceptional strength, courage, and unwavering loyalty. Suetake is often depicted as one of the 'Shitenno' (Four Heavenly Kings) of Yorimitsu, a group of elite warriors known for their supernatural abilities and bravery in battling demons and monsters. His origins are shrouded in some mystery, but he is believed to have come from the Urabe clan, known for their divinatory skills and warrior prowess. Suetake's most famous exploits involve confronting various Yokai, Oni, and other malevolent entities that plagued Kyoto during the Heian period. He wielded his katana with unmatched skill, often working alongside his fellow Shitenno, Watanabe no Tsuna, Sakata no Kintoki, and Usui Sadamitsu, to protect the capital and its inhabitants from supernatural threats. His dedication and bravery made him a symbol of loyalty and martial excellence, solidifying his place in Japanese legends as a true hero and paragon of Bushido. He embodies the ideals of a fearless warrior dedicated to protecting his lord and the innocent from evil. Suetake's stories continue to be retold and celebrated in Kabuki plays, Noh theater, and other forms of traditional Japanese art.
subtitle: Loyal warrior against demons and monsters.
achievements: Serving as one of the Shitenno

keyMyths.title: Defeating the Tsuchigumo
keyMyths.description: Urabe no Suetake is credited with helping to defeat the monstrous Tsuchigumo, a giant earth spider that terrorized the region. He used his skill and courage to overcome the creature's cunning and strength, ensuring the safety of the people.

keyMyths.title: Battles Against Oni
keyMyths.description: Suetake frequently fought against Oni, powerful demons that threatened Kyoto. His bravery and swordsmanship allowed him to vanquish these formidable foes, earning him the respect and gratitude of the populace.

keyMyths.title: Loyalty to Yorimitsu
keyMyths.description: Urabe no Suetake's unwavering loyalty to Minamoto no Yorimitsu is a central theme in his stories. He consistently put his lord's safety and well-being above his own, demonstrating the virtues of a true samurai.

sources.author: Helen Craig McCullough
sources.work: The Taiheiki: A Chronicle of Medieval Japan
sources.description: Provides historical context and mentions of warriors associated with Minamoto no Yorimitsu.
sources.text: Provides historical context and mentions of warriors associated with Minamoto no Yorimitsu.

sources.author: Richard Freeman
sources.work: The Kumaso Myth: A New Approach to Early Japanese Mythology
sources.description: Discusses the origins and significance of mythological creatures and heroes in Japanese folklore.
sources.text: Discusses the origins and significance of mythological creatures and heroes in Japanese folklore.

associations.name: Loyalty
associations.significance: Suetake's unwavering devotion to Minamoto no Yorimitsu is a core element of his character, embodying the samurai ideal of absolute fidelity.

associations.name: Courage
associations.significance: He consistently faces formidable foes and perilous situations without hesitation, highlighting his bravery.

associations.name: Strength
associations.significance: Suetake is frequently depicted as possessing superhuman strength, allowing him to overcome physical challenges and defeat monstrous enemies.

associations.name: Guardian
associations.significance: As one of the Shitenno, Suetake serves as a protector of Yorimitsu and the realm against supernatural threats.

associations.name: Sword
associations.significance: Like many samurai heroes, the sword is an extension of Suetake, symbolizing his martial prowess and commitment to justice.

associations.name: Mountain
associations.significance: Mountains represent the challenges and trials he overcomes, as well as the steadfastness and resilience required of a true warrior.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Both are renowned for their extraordinary strength and participation in legendary quests, battling monsters and performing superhuman feats.
Similar to Beowulf, Urabe no Suetake is a valiant warrior who confronts monstrous beings and protects his lord and people from danger.
Both are celebrated for their exceptional combat skills, unwavering loyalty, and roles as protectors of their respective realms, embodying the ideals of a warrior hero.

worshipPractices.name: Ancestor Veneration
worshipPractices.description: While not directly worshipped, Suetake, as a figure embodying samurai virtues, is honored through ancestor veneration practices within families claiming descent or affiliation with samurai clans. His deeds are recounted to instill values of loyalty and courage.

cultural.modernLegacy: Urabe no Suetake's legacy persists in Japanese popular culture, appearing in kabuki plays, noh theater, and modern media such as anime and video games. He represents the idealized samurai—brave, loyal, and skilled—a symbol of traditional Japanese values.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Origins
extendedContent.content: Details surrounding Urabe no Suetake's early life are scarce, shrouded in the mists of legend. He emerges into historical and folkloric prominence as a retainer of Minamoto no Yorimitsu, a powerful figure in the Heian period. It is believed that Suetake hailed from a family with a strong martial tradition, which would account for his exceptional combat skills and unwavering loyalty. Some accounts suggest he underwent rigorous training from a young age, mastering swordsmanship, archery, and other essential warrior arts. Regardless of the specific details, it is clear that Suetake possessed innate talent and a deep commitment to the warrior code, quickly rising through the ranks to become one of Yorimitsu's most trusted and formidable companions. His dedication and prowess set the stage for his later exploits and lasting fame as a legendary hero.

extendedContent.title: Greatest Quests and Battles
extendedContent.content: Urabe no Suetake is most famous for his participation in numerous legendary quests alongside Minamoto no Yorimitsu and the other Shitenno. One of his most notable exploits is the subjugation of Shuten-doji, a powerful oni (demon) who terrorized the region around Mount Oe. Suetake played a crucial role in infiltrating Shuten-doji's fortress and defeating the demon in a fierce battle. He also participated in the extermination of the Tsuchigumo, monstrous spider-like creatures that plagued the countryside. Tales recount Suetake's incredible strength and skill in combat, often depicting him wielding his sword with unmatched precision and power. These quests not only showcased his martial abilities but also his unwavering loyalty to Yorimitsu and his commitment to protecting the realm from supernatural threats. His bravery and selflessness cemented his reputation as a legendary hero.

extendedContent.title: Enduring Legacy
extendedContent.content: Urabe no Suetake's legacy as a paragon of samurai virtues continues to resonate in Japanese culture. He is remembered as a symbol of unwavering loyalty, courage, and martial prowess. His stories have been passed down through generations, inspiring countless individuals to emulate his dedication and selflessness. Suetake's image is frequently depicted in traditional art forms such as ukiyo-e prints, kabuki plays, and noh theater, further solidifying his place in the cultural consciousness. In modern times, he remains a popular figure in anime, manga, and video games, introducing his heroic tales to new audiences. Urabe no Suetake serves as a reminder of the enduring values of the samurai code and the importance of loyalty, courage, and self-sacrifice in the face of adversity. His story continues to inspire and captivate, ensuring that his legacy will endure for generations to come.

shortDescription: A loyal warrior of Minamoto no Yorimitsu, Urabe no Suetake is famed for his courage and strength as one of the Shitenno, battling demons and protecting the realm.
